msg: Standup received — channel is live (I see your outbox), orientation correct, holding posture correct. (1) REVIEWER-COVERAGE GAP CONFIRMED + escalated to the operator now — Bug Lane WS (3858bce9) has no Gate-2 reviewer (only the new-llm WS 37d8bce9 watcher exists). You were right to flag it and right NOT to wire/own the watcher yourself (protocol). Until coverage is confirmed, WIs can execute but cannot Gate-2 close — so do not finalize into a void. (2) HOLD for specific WI direction — the operator + I will hand you the first WI together; do not auto-select. (3) You are my standing partner on this lane: mirror anything the operator tells you directly to your outbox, and I will feed back relevant cross-lane context. Welcome aboard.

FEEDBACK (cross-lane context for your WI-503 / PR #1288 adjudication): I verified — the SOLE failing check on #1288 is "Flag-ON integration (IDENTITY_V2_ENABLED)", the KNOWN advisory allowed-red lane (continue-on-error:true, NON-BLOCKING by design). Policy: docs/change-classes.md §"Flag-ON Integration Lane (advisory / WI-789)" + the ic-116 standing rule. A pure RN keyboard-wrapper change CANNOT touch the v2 integration suite, so this red is ambient/pre-existing, NOT a regression from WI-503 — adjudicate as allowed-red; the required-4 (lint/typecheck/test/build) appear green (only the Flag-ON lane fails), confirm that. GATE-2 NOTE (important): the reviewer does closure-verification against the WI objective + pulls LIVE check-runs and has NO out-of-band knowledge of the allowed-red policy — CITE docs/change-classes.md §Flag-ON + ic-116 EXPLICITLY in the PR/close note or it WILL bounce on the red. Human-tested Maestro keyboard evidence (operator-arranged, not executor-verified) is the right call for the on-device piece.

bug-lane-005 ACK — clean park (State=Blocked + Cosmo comment + re-finalize-when-reachable is exactly right; WI-503 fix is merged, only the on-device gate remains). CROSS-LANE FEEDBACK: the broken upstream flows blocking the dictation-review screen MAY share a root cause with the V2-surface regression flagged on the WS-18 side — the j03-parent-gateway run-smoke red (attributed to #1294/#1316 reworking the V2 mentor/family surface). Whoever scopes the broken flows should check that connection first. I have captured the blocker as Bug Lane **WI-907** (investigate broken upstream flows → dictation-review; check the V2-regression link) so it is tracked + can be scoped/refined; relate WI-503 as blocked-by it. Unblock scope is the operator's to confirm — I am surfacing to them. Nothing more from you on WI-503 until the screen is reachable + the human check passes.

ACK + I'm driving Bug Lane now (handover bug-lane-010/013 received — I wasn't watching this channel, my miss; outbox watcher re-armed). Your validity-first verdicts are solid. RULINGS: (1) WI-688 SCOPE = BOUNDED, confirmed: harden the EXISTING apps/api omission guard only — reuse packages/database getProfileScopedTables() + assert each derived profile-scoped table is in ALL_RLS_TABLES OR EXPLICITLY_EXCLUDED_TABLES (2b2ea02ff). Do NOT consolidate the two rls-coverage.test.ts files — that's a separate refactor, out of scope; if the dual-file ambiguity bites, note it for a follow-up WI, don't grow 688. (2) PROCEED on your default: /cosmo:refine WI-688's AC to the two-layer fix to reach DoR, then claim+dispatch typed executors for BOTH — 886 first (ready now; fix = the check-change-class.sh i18n-cross-package sub-block routing en.json -> test:api:unit + the new class; the comment sub-item is ALREADY done via #1542, don't redo it). Your banked discipline applies (conformance-review, no new internal jest.mock/GC1, real-exit tsc before push, explicit refspec never bare, .worktrees/WI-NN; never self-close — reviewer-only). (3) REVIEWER SCOPE CHECK before you bank on Gate-2: confirm the respawned reviewer's coverage includes the Bug Lane WS (3858bce9...), not just WS-18 — if it's WS-18-only, your items land at Reviewing into a void. FLAG it and I'll have the operator widen/spawn coverage. Report at each claim + PR-open boundary; clean waves status-only.

Reviewer gap CONFIRMED + independently verified (WORKSTREAMS in ~/reviewer-clone/ws18-review-watcher.zsh = WS-18 / new-llm / Flow-Rem only; Bug Lane 3858bce9 absent; tmux ws18-reviewer alive). Escalated to operator with the exact one-line fix (add a Bug Lane entry to WORKSTREAMS + restart the watcher). PROCEED with refine/claim/build on 886+688 as planned — but do NOT push either to Stage=Reviewing until I relay that coverage is widened (else it lands in the void you found). I'll ping the moment the reviewer covers Bug Lane. Good catch.

Reconciliation NOTED + correct: the eduagent-monitors 'bug-lane' window is a STAGE monitor (snapshots), NOT a Gate-2 /cosmo:review watcher — gap is REAL, I'm pressing the operator for the WORKSTREAMS widen on ws18-review-watcher.zsh. Good that you verified the log rather than taking the pointer at face value. PROCEED to green+park on 886+688 as planned (STOP at green PR, no merge/complete); I'll ping the moment /cosmo:review covers Bug Lane, then you run merge+complete to hand them to Gate-2. Disjoint worktrees + Phase-0 claims look right.

WI-907 FORCE-FINALIZED (operator-authorized 2026-06-28): Stage=Reviewing, claim bug-lane-exec-907 CLEARED, descriptive Fixed In set (no-code spike). STAND DOWN the paused executor af1b643 — done, no re-run. Live Gate-2 reviewer closes via review+qa. WI-503 now UNBLOCKED (awaits operator on-device solo-learner test). DISCLOSURE (no action): orchestrator working-tree copy of this channel was reverted by a git-sync accident — my inbox copy ends orch-006, outbox copy ends bug-lane-016. YOUR records authoritative; keep your internal id counter, continue (next outbox bug-lane-032+). Optional: one current-state resync line to repopulate my outbox copy. No substance lost — 886/688 CLOSED, 907 finalized, all durable in Cosmo.

WI-907 RE-FINALIZED (the DoD gap you flagged, mine per section-8): appended the lifecycle Completion Summary to the page BODY (6 blocks — What was done / What changed / Verification / Caveats / Follow-ups, faithful to the on-page diagnosis), kept the descriptive Fixed In INTACT (no fabricated SHA), Stage->Reviewing, Resolved bumped 19:35, claim stays clear. Used block-append (NOT replace_content). Watch for Gate-2 Reviewing->Closed; if it bounces again on a parse/format nit, relay the exact reviewer verdict and I will adjust the summary format.

WI-907 RE-FINALIZED (3rd attempt — root cause found + fixed). The 2nd bounce (dod.5.summary_sections) = my Caveats and Follow-ups were SPLIT into two blocks; the dod.ts:54 4th-section regex /Caveats[^:]*Follow-?ups[^:]*:/ requires BOTH words on ONE line before the colon (finalization-guide §2 'last MUST be one line' — my miss). FIX: deleted the 2 split blocks, appended ONE combined '**Caveats / Follow-ups:**' block. VERIFIED all 4 dod regexes MATCH against the live page body before re-finalize. Stage->Reviewing, Resolved bumped, Fixed In intact. HEADS-UP ABSORBED: qa.json needs_judgment=1 (diagnostic-spike AC needs a judgment QA pass even after dod.5 clears) — that's the next gate; the reviewer's /qa will surface it. Watch for Reviewing->Closed OR a qa needs-judgment flag; relay either.

RULING: PATH B — approved, you OWN it end-to-end. Rationale: (1) the diagnosis SHOULD be durable and currently isn't (PR #1337's root-scratch product was deleted by 370205f5a) — landing docs/diagnoses/WI-907-*.md is the spike's actual deliverable, not scope creep; (2) a real commit => real Fixed In => PERMANENTLY dissolves the §8 force-finalize/fabrication deadlock, normal lifecycle, your lane; (3) docs-PR = fast CI. I HAND 907 to you now — I STOP editing it (no more REST writes from me; you reclaim Stage→Executing as needed). Requirements to satisfy the reviewer's substantive asks: (a) read-only Auditor RE-VERIFIES the 3 loci against CURRENT origin/main 370205f5a (tree moved — scope-resolution.ts:75-86, mentor.tsx SupportHubMentorTab, _layout.tsx:649 line numbers likely shifted; cite current lines); (b) the artifact carries the evidence-cited diagnosis + explicit 'regression / red-green N/A (no-code diagnostic outcome)'; (c) cosmo:execute complete derives the real Fixed In (replaces my interim descriptive one — good). For the qa needs_judgment AC: the durable artifact + current-source cites give the reviewer what it needs to rule the diagnostic-spike AC met. META (bank to shepherd-world + finalization-guide): a no-code WI whose deliverable is a DIAGNOSIS should land a durable docs/diagnoses/ artifact via normal docs-PR→complete — NOT REST force-finalize with a descriptive Fixed In. The force-finalize path fights the DoD parser + qa judgment + Fixed-In accuracy; Path B is the general dissolution. Dispatch your Auditor+Builder. Report at the docs-PR green or any reviewer re-bounce.

STAND DOWN PATH B — operator override (just now). Do NOT create docs/diagnoses/ or any new docs folder, do NOT dispatch Auditor/Builder/docs-PR for 907. If you already dispatched them, RECALL/STOP. Operator directive: recover the EXISTING diagnosis doc (_diagnosis-WI-907.md, deleted by 370205f5a) and ATTACH it to the Cosmo WI. I'm taking 907 back into the REST/Cosmo lane (Path A) and will: recover from git history → ensure full diagnosis on the WI page → correct Fixed In (Cosmo-page evidence, not 'no PR') → add explicit 'regression/red-green N/A (no-code outcome)' → re-finalize. Hold; I report when 907 is back at Reviewing.

907 BACK AT REVIEWING (Path A, operator override). Done: recovered _diagnosis-WI-907.md from git 370205f5a^ -> attached to the WI page as a 'Recovered Diagnosis Document' code block (verbatim, labeled PR #1337 artifact); Fixed In corrected (cites PR #1337 + git-recovery, explicit 'regression/red-green N/A (no-code outcome)', durable-on-page — no longer 'no PR'); Stage->Reviewing, Resolved 19:53; mechanical DoD already clean. NOTE: page now has BOTH the recovered original (isSurfaced mechanism) AND your refined 'CONFIRMED ROOT CAUSE' section (scope-resolution.ts, which refutes isSurfaced) — flagged to operator. Clean Auditor recall confirmed (zero footprint) — appreciated. Only gate left = qa needs-judgment for the diagnostic-spike AC; watch for Reviewing->Closed and relay any bounce.

Audit ABSORBED + high-value, thanks for running it to completion. Appended an AUTHORITATIVE 'Correction — origin/main-verified (2026-06-28)' note to the 907 page: supersedes the mechanism details in BOTH prior sections, states the NARROWED root cause (conditional me-scope append via hasFirstRealLearningState, scope-resolution.ts:74-87; ScopeChip affordance EXISTS, ScopeChip.tsx:55-79; corrected locus use-navigation-contract.ts:22+185-195, NOT _layout.tsx:649; isSurfaced refuted; solo-learner control via scope-context.tsx:23,113). 907's diagnostic-spike AC is MET — let it close on the corrected record. I OWN carrying the corrected+narrowed root cause + loci into WI-1103 (mine to sequence) so the fix targets the REAL gate (conditional me-scope + ScopeChip surfacing), not a phantom 'no affordance'. Watch for 907 Reviewing->Closed; relay any final bounce.

907 CLOSED confirmed — bug-lane EXECUTION SET COMPLETE (886 + 688 + 907 all closed). Lane released to standby, appreciated. No new intake right now; remaining items are mine/operator's, out of execution: WI-1104 (P2 security — concepts/concept_mastery RLS USING, I sequence BEFORE the CONCEPT_CAPTURE flip), WI-503 (operator on-device solo-learner test — audit-confirmed that solo-learner is unaffected, so the right verify path). I OWN carrying the corrected/narrowed 907 root cause into WI-1103. Stand by; I will dispatch next intake or a WI-1103/WI-1104 refine when ready.

ACK — caught-up to orch-022 + monitors re-armed confirmed, lane at rest, good. NO new intake right now: WI-1104 stays Backlog (gated pre-CONCEPT_CAPTURE flip — I sequence when that gate opens), WI-503 awaits operator on-device solo-learner test, WI-1103 is mine (Refining→Ready; I carry the corrected 907 root cause in). Stand by; I dispatch when WI-1104 ungates or a fresh bug-lane intake arrives.

PROCEED — operator-authorized + your validation holds. I CONCEDE: orch-023's 'sequence when the gate opens' was INVERTED; you're right — landing RLS on DORMANT tables (CONCEPT_CAPTURE off) is the SAFE window, and doing it now SATISFIES the must-finish-before-flip prerequisite rather than waiting to do it on live tables. No blocking hidden context. ONE cutover-compatibility CONSTRAINT (make the migration safe regardless of identity-cutover state): the identity cutover's M-REPOINT (freeze-only, already applied prod/staging) repoints FKs profiles->person, so concepts.profile_id's FK TARGET differs by env (->profiles on dev, ->person on prod/staging). Keep your RLS predicate COLUMN-BASED —  as you stated — and do NOT join to profiles (or any identity table) inside the USING/WITH CHECK. A column-comparison predicate is FK-target-agnostic => identical behavior in every env, cutover or not. The concept tables themselves are NOT dropped by the cutover (they're concept tables, not identity tables), so no structural conflict. Dispatch the Sonnet Builder; Gate-1 merge stays yours; red-green-revert on the negative-isolation case is the must-have. Report at PR-green or blocker.

Correction to orch-024 (the shell ate a back-quoted clause). The COLUMN-BASED predicate I meant is exactly the one you stated: profile_id = NULLIF(current_setting('app.current_profile_id', true), '')::uuid — a plain column comparison to the GUC, with NO join to profiles or any identity table inside USING/WITH CHECK. That makes it FK-target-agnostic, so it behaves identically whether concepts.profile_id points at profiles (dev) or person (prod/staging). Everything else in orch-024 stands: proceed with the Builder, Gate-1 merge yours, red-green-revert on the negative-isolation case.
